snippet.title: Hans Vestberg | The 2023 GW Business & Policy Forum
snippet.description:
Hans Vestberg, Chairman and CEO of Verizon, discusses digital inclusion and how it could be addressed. 

About the 2023 GW Business & Policy Forum
Emerging technologies have become a dominant force in our society — driving economics, policy and culture, and accounting for more than US$40 trillion in global nominal GDP. On April 25, 2023, those driving forces became the focus of global leaders from both the private and public sectors as they came together at the George Washington University for the first annual GW Business & Policy Forum. The theme for this inaugural forum was Attacking Cybersecurity Risks. Participants gained invaluable insights and opportunities for interaction as we addressed the most significant issues related to this ever-evolving landscape and the profound implications they have for global business, markets and regulation.

snippet.title: Lessons in leadership we can all learn from: celebrating 100 episodes of Meet the Leader
snippet.description:
This week we’re celebrating 100 episodes of our sister podcast Meet the Leader.
 

Every week, Linda Lacina interviews leaders - of major companies, organisations, or what we might call ‘thought leaders' in the fields of academia or campaign groups.
 

If you want to know what makes these individuals tick, and what lessons we might learn from their experiences, subscribe to Meet the Leader (https://www.weforum.org/podcasts/meet-the-leader)  - you can find it on our podcast website, wef.ch/podcasts (https://www.weforum.org/podcasts)  and on any podcast app (https://pod.link/1534915560) .
 

In this episode:
 

Jane Goodall, Founder, Jane Goodall Institute; Al Gore, Founder, Climate Reality Project; former US Vice President; Hans Vestberg, CEO, Verizon; Bas Van Abel, Founder, Fairphone; Punit Renjen, Global CEO Emeritus, Deloitte; Caroline Casey, Founder, The Valuable 500; Harmony Jade Wayner, International Arctic Research Center; Andrea Fuder, Chief Procurement Officer, Volvo Group; Yuxiang Zhou, Founder, Black Lake Technologies; John Amaechi, Founder, APS Intelligence.

snippet.title: The Business Case for Second Chance Employment
snippet.description:
At the Business Case for Second Chance Employment, Charting a Path Forward With Business Schools and Corporations, Damien Dwin, CEO of Lafayette Square, and Hans Vestberg, CEO of Verizon, discussed the business case for second chance employment. This session was moderated by Stephanie Ruhle, host of “The 11th Hour” at MSNBC and senior business analyst at NBC News.

This conference was organized by the Business Roundtable; Tamer Center for Social Enterprise at Columbia Business School; Justice Through Code at Columbia University; and Second Chance Business Coalition, co-chaired by JPMorgan Chase and Eaton Corporation. It explored labor market trends and the role that corporations and business schools can play in educating the business community and future leaders on the benefits of second chance employment.

snippet.title: Unbelievable Spot Robot #ai #bostondynamics #robot coperni fall winter 2023
snippet.description: Boston Dynamics' Spot is a four-legged robot, also known as a robotdog, designed for industrial inspections, security patrols, and search and rescue missions. It has advanced sensors, can climb stairs, traverse rough terrain, and recover from being pushed. It is highly customizable with various payload options and has been used in various industries alongside its sibling robot Atlas.

snippet.title: How Data Can Fuel Business Growth I TechTrends I J.P. Morgan

snippet.title: Ericsson Wallet Platform: Building a brighter financial future
snippet.description:
https://www.ericsson.com/en/mobile-financial-services
Ericsson imagined a digitally connected financially inclusive world, where every individual and business has access to convenient, affordable, and secure financial services. Ericsson Wallet Platform turned this imagination into reality by combining the powers of mobile communication and financial services to offer mobile money, digital wallet, and payment services.

Ericsson Wallet Platform is a fintech platform that enables telecom operators and financial service providers to offer fast, secure, personalized, and easy-to-use mobile financial services to their customers. The platform drives financial inclusion and well-being by enabling all consumers, banked or unbanked, to transfer, pay, save, grow, receive, and borrow money.

Ericsson Wallet Platform supports over 300 million mobile wallets and processes more than 2.6 billion transactions worth over USD 36 billion every month. With an open architecture offering a wide range of APIs for mobile financial service providers and their partners, the wallet platform provides a full suite of technology, deployment, and operational services to launch and scale mobile financial services creating a brighter financial future for everyone.

Find out more about the Ericsson Wallet Platform: https://www.ericsson.com/en/mobile-financial-services
